text: The surgeon uses a high-energy device to achieve hemostasis while dissecting adhesions around the lung parenchyma. The surgical field reveals the pulmonary artery branches to the lower lobe surrounded by enlarged lymph nodes.

The surgeon identifies a markedly enlarged bronchial artery in the posterior aspect of the pulmonary hilum, behind the apical segmental artery. After completing the section, the vessel is sutured using a stapling device. Another pathological bronchial artery within the pulmonary ligament is isolated and closed with a high energy device, facilitating the safe isolation and ligation of the lower pulmonary vein.

The surgeon completes the dissection of the fissure and closes the bronchus. The resected specimen is placed into an endo bag for extraction. A single chest tube is inserted through the posterior axis at the end of the procedure.
